title: How to Reset Your Active Directory / SSO Password
text:
Q: How do I reset my Active Directory or SSO password?
A: Windows: Step 1: Press Ctrl + Alt + Delete and select 'Change a password'. Step 2: Enter your current password, then your new password, and confirm it. Step 3: Click the arrow or press Enter to finalize the change. If you are unable to log in, you can also reset your password via the password reset portal by navigating to https://passwordreset.company.com. macOS: Step 1: Open 'System Preferences' and click on 'Users & Groups'. Step 2: Click on your account and select 'Reset Password'. Step 3: Enter your current password, then your new password, and confirm it. Step 4: Click 'Change Password' to complete the process. If you are locked out, visit the password reset portal at https://passwordreset.company.com to reset your password. Remember, your new password must be at least 8 characters long and include a mix of letters, numbers, and special characters.
